mnemonics: SLOW Changes: Type 1 (Slowly Changing Dimension) overwrites old data, Type 2 adds new row with versioning, Type 3 adds new column for previous value.
keyPoints: Type 1 (Overwrite): Simple update, loses historical data.
Type 2 (Add Row): Creates a new record with a surrogate key, effective date, and often a version number to preserve history.
Type 3 (Add Column): Adds a new column to store the previous value, preserving only one previous state.
commonMistakes: Using Type 1 when historical tracking is required, leading to loss of data history.
Applying Type 2 or Type 3 unnecessarily for attributes that do not need historical tracking, adding complexity and storage costs.
Forgetting to include effective date columns or version flags when implementing Type 2 SCDs.
